claim: The attention core shall name no agent-platform token (Claude/Anthropic/hook/model name) so a platform adapter is one consumer, never the owner.
why: Resolver verdict (2026-07-03): 'make the solution universal for any agent, and at the seam make an API for Claude'. The core must therefore know nothing of any one agent platform -- the platform adapter is a thin wrapper, not part of the core. This is the same backend-neutrality stance as R-core-imports-stdlib-or-hotam-spec-only, extended with a claim-specific platform-token scan. Not yet implemented: the attention layer is Planned (previously the core lived in src/hotam_spec/attention.py, the adapter in tools/attention_hook.py, guarded by tests/test_attention_core.py::test_core_names_no_platform_token).
